title: Endurance: Shackleton's Incredible Voyage
author: Alfred Lansing
content: “The task was not to save the mission, but to preserve the unit.”
action: Record: The prioritization of system integrity over original objective goals when the environment becomes non-survivable.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor Frankl
content: “He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.”
action: Record: The identification of internal purpose as the fundamental psychological buffer against extreme systemic stress.

title: Into Thin Air
author: Jon Krakauer
content: “The higher you go, the more the rules of the game change.”
action: Record: Acknowledging that technical competence at base level does not translate to competence in extreme, high-risk scenarios.

title: The Right Stuff
author: Tom Wolfe
content: “The right stuff... meant that you were willing to push it to the very edge.”
action: Record: The cultivation of high-variance, high-reward decision-making as a deliberate survival trait.

title: Survival in the Killing Fields
author: Haing Ngor
content: “To keep your identity is to keep your life.”
action: Record: Maintaining internal cognitive structure as the primary defense against systemic eradication.
